QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_11

question.text: The approximate Yield to Maturity (YTM) formula is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** YTM approximation = [Coupon + (Face Value - Price)/years to maturity] / [(Face Value + Price)/2]. This considers both coupon income and capital gain/loss. **The Trap:** Coupon/FV is the coupon rate. Market Price/EPS is the PE ratio. Dividend/Market Price is the dividend yield. Only the YTM formula accounts for all bond cash flows. **Cross-Exam Context:** YTM calculation is extensively tested in NISM Series XV and IV (Interest Rate Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** YTM is the most comprehensive single measure of bond return. It assumes all coupons are reinvested at the same rate — the reinvestment risk assumption.
